rationale: The "relaxing bus jam" satisfaction hook is a puzzle/sort archetype that fundamentally misrepresents Roof Rails' fast-paced runner core loop — there are no rails, pole-balancing, or forward motion in the hook, so installs would be heavily mismatched and D1 retention would suffer. Palette is adjacent-casual but the coral/teal mix clashes with the game's signature cyan-and-yellow rooftop aesthetic, and the "relaxed" emotional pitch directly contradicts the game's energetic/competitive UI mood. Audience overlap (broad casual, all ages/genders) is the only real bridge; recommend skipping unless we test a hybrid where the bus-clearing hook hard-cuts at 3s into authentic rail-sliding gameplay.
